summary.intent: Add live integration tests for Fireworks, OpenAI, and Anthropic providers, following the pattern established for MiniMax and Moonshot.
summary.outcome: Fireworks (8/9 passing, 1 transient rate limit), Anthropic (9/9 passing after case-insensitive fix), and OpenAI (model discovery passed, live tests blocked by insufficient quota) integration tests were written and committed.

repo: MiniMax silently ignores the top-level `system` field; system content must be prepended to the first user message.
Moonshot's `reasoning_content` field for multi-turn tool use requires the original reasoning from step 1, which `ChatMessage` doesn't carry — a known structural limitation.
Fireworks is a standard OpenAI-compatible provider with no quirks; model discovery is enabled and returned 7 models, but only 3 were alive at test time.
OpenAI model discovery found 102 models not in the catalog.
Some LLM providers (e.g., Anthropic) may not preserve keyword casing in responses, requiring case-insensitive assertions in tests.

code.finding: Initial test model (llama-v3p1-70b-instruct) was dead; replaced with a working model after catalog check revealed only 3/7 models alive.
System prompt keyword assertion failed because Anthropic lowercased the response; fixed by making all keyword checks case-insensitive across all provider test files.

workflow: Running a full test suite against a free-tier API account can exhaust rate limits mid-run, causing transient 429 failures on later tests (especially multi-turn).
Case-insensitive keyword assertions should be the default pattern for all provider integration tests to avoid false negatives from model response normalization.
Catalog/model discovery tests are valuable for detecting provider-side model deprecations before they affect users.

friction: Fireworks free-tier rate limit caused a 429 on the multi-turn test, requiring manual cooldown and retry.
Fireworks default test model was dead, requiring a mid-session fix after the catalog check surfaced the issue.
OpenAI account had insufficient credits, blocking all live inference tests despite correct test code.
Anthropic system prompt test failed initially due to unexpected lowercasing behavior, requiring a case-insensitive fix across all test files.
open_items: GitHub Copilot integration tests are not written — OAuth device flow requires special handling beyond a plain API key.
OpenAI live inference tests remain untested due to insufficient account quota; needs credits to validate.
Fireworks multi-turn test correctness is assumed but not confirmed against the live API due to rate limiting.
Moonshot `reasoning_content` multi-turn tool use limitation is documented but not resolved — `ChatMessage` would need to carry reasoning content to fix it.
13 new Moonshot models discovered in `/models` endpoint are not yet added to the catalog.
